context_summary: Phase 19.2 verified PASS-WITH-DEFICIT (8/8 plans complete). Live trace investigation identified the deficit's root cause: WindowedOp bypass of Plan 19.2-01's pre-extraction. 60 of 88 fraud-team feature updates pay ~100 ns wrapping tax = ~9000 ns/event of the 14 µs agg-stage budget. Three Phase 19.3 sub-goals proposed (95k → 107k → 125k EPS stacked). GSD upgraded 1.34.2 → 1.38.5 + model_profile switched to quality + system table customized so all 18 GSD agents resolve to opus. Next: open Phase 19.3, decide whether to update 19.2-VERIFICATION.md correction, save WindowedOp bypass memory.

decisions.rationale: Apply-loop microbench wins are real and confirmed (UDDSketch 1.55x, EventTypeMix 45x). End-to-end EPS shortfall on fraud-team explained by WindowedOp bypass + recipe-op cost shift; not a regression in apply-path work.

decisions.decision: WindowedOp pre-extraction is THE Phase 19.3 priority
decisions.rationale: Live trace shows agg=14µs not 362ns; 60 of 88 fraud-team feature updates are windowed and pay row.get() linear scan + double-dispatch tax; predicted -3,900 ns/event from update_at fast path alone.

Verifier conjectured without running BEAVA_TRACE_APPLY_TIMING. Trace shows wal_append=85ns (0.6%), bookkeeping=337ns (2.2%). Real bottleneck is agg=14µs. Future Performance Discipline phases must include live trace, not just criterion bench.

decisions.decision: All 18 GSD agents on opus under quality profile
decisions.rationale: Phase 19.2 surfaced multiple sonnet-executor + sonnet-verifier + sonnet-plan-checker bypass patterns (deferred punts, todo!() placeholders, conjecture without measurement). User upgrade request applied at project + system level.

anti_patterns_discovered.name: WindowedOp bypass of dispatch-protocol refactor
anti_patterns_discovered.description: Plan 19.2-01 said 'all aggs use field_idx (~5 ns vs ~30-60 ns)' but only migrated non-windowed dispatch arms. WindowedOp::update_with_row receives raw row + re-does row.get(fname) inside each bucket update. 60 of 88 fraud-team feature updates pay ~100 ns wrapping tax = ~9000 ns/event. Caught only by post-execution live trace investigation, not by plan-checker or verifier.
anti_patterns_discovered.structural_mitigation: Dispatch-protocol refactors must enumerate ALL dispatch entry points up-front: top-level + nested wrappers (WindowedOp, RetractableOp, etc.). Plan acceptance criteria use exhaustive grep checks (e.g., grep -c 'row.get(' returns ≤ 5) not 'callsites collapsed'.

anti_patterns_discovered.name: Verifier conjecture without measurement
anti_patterns_discovered.description: Phase 19.2-08 verifier wrote 'WAL+bookkeeping overhead dominates the per-event budget' as the diagnosis for the EPS deficit, without running BEAVA_TRACE_APPLY_TIMING. Trace later showed WAL is 85 ns/event (0.6%); the diagnosis was wrong. Misdirected Phase 19.3 priority toward WAL/group-commit work that wouldn't have helped.
anti_patterns_discovered.structural_mitigation: Performance Discipline phases must include live trace runs in verification, not just criterion microbench acceptance. CONTEXT.md predicted lift table must be confirmed against actual per-stage measurements.

anti_patterns_discovered.name: Sonnet executor declares done with deferred punts
anti_patterns_discovered.description: Across Phase 19.2 plans: Plan 19.2-01 deferred geo field_idx to non-existent follow-up; Plan 19.2-05 deferred EventTypeMix migration; Plan 19.2-06 entropy.rs accessors with todo!() placeholder + 'if absent' hedge. Plan-checker caught some; verifier missed the WindowedOp one entirely.
anti_patterns_discovered.structural_mitigation: model_profile=quality with all-18-agents on opus; plan-checker should reject 'deferred / TODO / if absent' language as structural smell.

context_notes: The big surprise this session: verifier's WAL bottleneck conjecture was wrong, and the actual cause (WindowedOp bypass of pre-extraction) was identifiable via a 30-second live trace that wasn't in the verification protocol. Phase 19.2 still verified PASS-WITH-DEFICIT (microbench wins are real); but the EPS gap stays open until Phase 19.3 extends Plan 19.2-01's update_at protocol to WindowedOp wrapper layer. The model profile change (all agents on opus) is a structural fix for the broader pattern of bypasses observed across the phase — sonnet executors declare done when explicit acceptance criteria pass, without checking whether the architectural intent extends to wrapper layers the plan didn't enumerate. Opus does broader pattern matching.
